title: gymnosporia albata
intro: Gymnosporia albata, also known as the White Milk Bush, is a shrub-like plant species native to South Africa. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and inflammation, and treating various skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. The herbal preparations of Gymnosporia albata typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, which are then used to treat various ailments.
